passages.passage_text: Directions: A statement is given followed by several assumptions. An assumption is something assumed or taken for granted. Read the statement carefully and decide which of the assumptions is implicit in the statement.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "Join X coaching classes - we guarantee you success." - an advertisement
Assumptions: I. Such coaching classes are well-equipped.
II. Students joining coaching classes expect success.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "If he not mend his ways, I will call the police".
Assumptions: I. He may mend his ways.
II. The police may help me.
III. He has been making hoax calls to me.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "Do not touch stray objects. They may be bombs." - a notice by City Police
Assumptions: I. Stray objects are harmless.
II. Stray objects may be touched by people.
III. People will take notice of the warning.

choices.4: I and either II or III are implicit
choices.5: At least one of the above is implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "Medu, is a Head of A dog." - A tells B about Madhu.
Assumptions: I. B knows Madhu.
II. Some people do not bark like dogs.

choices.5: Either I or II and III are implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "The prices should fall after May. By that time the demand of foodgrains starts dropping." - an economist
Assumptions: I. Prices will fall after May.
II. The economist is in line with farmers of the country.
III. A drop in demand (of foodgrains) will result in a drop in supply (of foodgrains).

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "The Finance Commission must grant more funds to our state. After all, we are the biggest suppliers of ores to the rest of India." - the CM of a state

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assumptions: I. The Finance Commission is not in a mood to grant more funds to the state.
II. More funds should be given to states that supply ores.
III. The Chief Minister has already written to the Finance Commission asking for more funds.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "I do not like consumer goods as a wedding gift. I prefer to give cash." - A tells B.
Assumptions: A is invited at weddings.
II. Some people give consumer goods as wedding gifts.
III. Some people give useless things as wedding gifts.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "The newspaper is expected to announce an expansion in its ministry shortly." - a newspaper report
Assumptions: I. The newspaper has reliable sources for the news.
II. The Prime Minister has the power to expand the cabinet.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "You cannot solve such questions without consulting a diagram. Difficult questions need diagrams for solving." - A teacher tells his students
Assumptions: I. The students are not intelligent.
II. The problem cannot be solved.
III. Such questions are difficult.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "You should not drink whisky. You must take care of your health." - A tells his friend.
Assumptions: I. A knows about the ill-effects of whisky.
II. Whisky is intoxicating.
III. Whisky is not good for health.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "I have decided to cut down on coffee. People tell me that caffeine is not good for health." - A tells B.
Assumptions: I. Coffee contains caffeine.
II. People are correct in their views about coffee.
III. B may also cut down on coffee.
